# API Contracts Checklist: PRSG-009 multi-PR emission

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-009 API, CLI, schema, PR-body, and generated-MOC contracts are complete, clear, measurable, and ready for implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are per-slice PR body inputs defined without breaking the existing positional `generate-pr-body.sh` invocation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015a, Plan §Declared File Operations]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define the required per-slice PR body output sections or labels produced from a slice packet, not only the packet input fields? [Resolved: Spec §Session 4 and FR-015d define stable output sections; Plan §API Contract Decisions mirrors them.]
- [x] CHK003 Does the layer-plan input contract name the PRSG-008 envelope and increment fields that PRSG-009 consumes for ordering, branch names, file scope, and scoped tests? [Resolved: Spec §FR-001a and Data Model §Layer Plan map PRSG-008 envelope and increment fields.]
- [ ] CHK004 Are `restack.sh` required arguments and mutation semantics specified for dry-run and `--apply` modes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017b, Quickstart §6]
- [ ] CHK005 Are generated Spec MOC PR table row fields specified for reviewer navigation and resume evidence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §PRS Manifest v2]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Are explicit `gh pr create --base --head --body-file` requirements unambiguous enough to avoid GitHub CLI default base/head inference? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004a]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the slice branch naming contract precise about padding width, slug source, and ref validation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007a]
- [x] CHK008 Are `restack.sh` success and failure exit codes assigned concrete numeric values instead of only category names? [Resolved: Spec §FR-017c, Plan §API Contract Decisions, Quickstart §6, and restack-output schema all define the numeric map.]
- [x] CHK009 Is the `restack.sh` stderr diagnostic format specific enough to be deterministic and testable? [Resolved: Spec §FR-017d, Data Model §Restack Operation, and Quickstart §6 define deterministic stderr format.]
- [ ] CHK010 Are PRS v2 SHA semantics clear for open vs merged PR rows? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008b, Contract §prs-v2.schema.json]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Does the plan consistently keep scoped CI as local recorded evidence rather than GitHub Actions workflow changes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015b, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK012 Are slice packet fields consistent across spec requirements, data model, and `slice-packet.schema.json`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015a, Data Model §Slice Packet]
- [ ] CHK013 Are PRS v2 row fields consistent across spec requirements, data model, and `prs-v2.schema.json`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §PRS Manifest v2]
- [ ] CHK014 Does the plan consistently preserve Claude/Codex parity for behavior-bearing references? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK015 Does PRSG-009 consistently defer new slicing or routing heuristics to PRSG-010? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Constraints]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can the three-slice emission outcome be objectively verified against PRSG-008 order and Style B bases? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK017 Can failed-slice stop behavior be objectively verified before any `gh pr create` call for that slice? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Can each slice PR's review packet requirements be measured against declared output fields and evidence paths? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the required fresh default verification after applied restack represented as a measurable post-restack gate? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Clarifications Session 3, Plan §Verification Gates]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are missing, unreadable, empty, invalid, and warning-bearing layer-plan scenarios covered with distinct requirements? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Layer Plan]
- [ ] CHK021 Are branch/PR resume scenarios covered for local branches, remote branches, open PRs, closed PRs, and merged PRs? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK022 Are zero and multiple GitHub PR reconciliation matches defined as stop conditions before duplicate PR creation? [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications Session 2]
- [ ] CHK023 Are single-slice plans covered by the same multi-PR emission contract rather than the legacy flattened path? [Coverage, Spec §US1 Acceptance Scenario 2]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK024 Does the spec define what happens when the PR body slice packet is missing required fields or fails schema validation? [Resolved: Spec §FR-015c and Data Model §Slice Packet define exit 2, stderr, and no-write behavior.]
- [x] CHK025 Does the spec define how PRSG-009 handles a PRSG-008 layer-plan envelope with `status` other than `ok` or with warnings present? [Resolved: Spec §FR-001b and Data Model §Layer Plan define blocking non-ok statuses and preserving ok warnings.]
- [ ] CHK026 Are stale or schema v1 `.process/prs.json` manifests covered without breaking backward-compatible rendering? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK027 Are state-persistence failures immediately after GitHub PR creation covered with enough recovery evidence? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Clarifications Session 1]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK028 Are external CLI dependencies (`git`, `gh`, `jq`, optional `gh-stack`) documented with required and optional roles? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK029 Are GitHub CLI PR listing/view fields needed for reconciliation documented through the durable PR record requirements? [Dependency, Spec §FR-011a, Data Model §Slice Record]
- [ ] CHK030 Is the PRSG-008 layer-plan schema source stable after archive cleanup and referenced from the vendored fixture location? [Dependency, Memory §Layer-planner]
